entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Process insights to process intelligence
entry.content:
Hi @Manish_Srimathula ,

thanks for your request and sorry for the delay of our response!

As it seems like you have SAP Signavio Process Insights running (live and data in it) and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence licensed, you can ease your SAP Signavio Process Intelligence Go-Live by using our Plug and Gain approach. SAP Signavio Plug and Gain lets you combine the capabilities of SAP Signavio Process Insights and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to reduce the time needed to thoroughly prepare your transformation from months to just hours, improve the deployment quality, and provide benefits to the business.

Here, you can find helpful resources to getting started with SAP Signavio Plug and Gain:

  • Blogpost - General Guide: Getting started with PnG: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/general-guide-getting-started-with-the-sap-signavio-plug-and-gain-approach/ba-p/13942139
  • Blogpost - Technical Guide: Getting started with PnG: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/technical-guide-getting-started-with-the-sap-signavio-plug-and-gain/ba-p/13934261
  • Plug and Gain Delivery Request Form: https://me.sap.com/notes/3529579
  • Notes PnG Value Accelerators Packages: https://me.sap.com/notes/3496399/E

In case, you want to jump on a call, feel free to shoot me a message via ascha.ahmed@sap.com 

Kind regards

Ascha (as part of the virtual Customer Success Team)

entry.title: Re: Depreciation start date is taking month beginning dat...
entry.content:
See period control in help https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/8865d153c9684608e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

And dates section https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/9736dd51e2193946e10000000a44538d.html?locale=en-US

With LINS, which depreciates using 12 periods, this is expected.

entry.title: Re: In asset master data quantity field need to be editab...
entry.content:
Quantity in the asset master data does not work like that, there is no standard way to do a comparison. 

Quantity is managed on the asset with ledger postings, it is not an editable field. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/27823c8ce56b4408a38c6f8867e4856c.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Error when deploying Fiori app from VS code -Unexpect...
entry.content: There was some issue with my server. Basis corrected it and this error is gone. Still working on the other errors during deployment. Thank you for your answer! Appreciate it!

entry.title: Re: Settlement does not recognize Downpayment of the prev...
entry.content: In the AUC, have you enabled the negative indicator for each depreciation area?

entry.title: Re: How to block response message when using Parallel Multicast
entry.content: did you manage to solve it?

entry.title: Re: Principal Propagation in SAP Integration Suite from external system to an SAP Backend
entry.content:
Thanks Francisco for the details,

From the comment above:  If OAuth or Client Certificate is used, then in Integration Suite  we use Service Keys, which are not associated with a user identity. In that case, SAP APIM is used to generate a SAML artifact which is exchanged with XSUAA for a token. 

Question: Is it possible to replace SAP APIM with Azure APIM instead? 

Regards,

Abhinav

 

 

 

Instead

entry.title: HU warehouse task for outbound scenario
entry.content:
Hi All,

Apologies for basic question.

Like in inbound once you pack it system will create a HU warehouse task. is there any provision where during picking if my delivery qty is 10 and in warehouse system found a HU with 10 qty and auto warehouse task created successfully. But its creating a product warehouse task not HU warehouse task.

Please let me know if this standard behavior or any config is there to create HU warehouse task for outbound

entry.title: Join us at the Integration Modernization Summit for SAP Partners
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, the need for organizations to have an agile and scalable integration architecture has become more critical than ever before. As technology continues to evolve, modernizing integration architecture becomes crucial for organizations to stay competitive. In the SAP ecosystem partners play an important role helping organizations to unlock the full potential of modern integration technologies SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) provides.

Hence, we would like to call all SAP partners to join us for the Integration Modernization Summit for SAP Partners to get valuable insights and updates about SAP's integration strategy, SAP’s integration portfolio, SAP’s best practices for integration and SAP’s offerings for partners. This event is scheduled for January 27th and 28th 2025 at SAP in St. Leon-Rot (Germany). The agenda of this event is designed to meet the needs of different SAP partner types such as sell, build and service partners.

Let’s take a look at the preliminary agenda of this summit:

Explore integration modernization and business opportunities with SAP Integration Suite

We plan to kick-off the first day in the early afternoon with lectures to set the stage for the entire event:

  • Integrate to innovate - SAP’s integration strategy
  • Bridging the gap - Exploring the untapped potential for integration
  • Uplevel your integration along your ERP transformation

In the afternoon we  plan to offer further sessions allowing you to learn from SAP experts about how sell, service and implement SAP Integration Suite for modernizing integration. We plan to close the day with an evening event which will offer a lot of networking opportunities in a relaxed atmosphere.

Enjoy deep dive into sessions on integration modernization

Let’s start the second day of the event with lectures about SAP partner program offerings, integration best practices and product innovations, such as:

  • Empowering Partner Success - programs & resources for SAP Partners
  • Defining and executing an integration strategy based on ISA-M using SAP Integration Suite together with SAP LeanIX enterprise architecture management solutions
  • Future innovations planned for SAP Integration Suite and SAP Datasphere

In the afternoon you will have the opportunity to join various breakout sessions to learn more about how to sell, transform and innovate with SAP Integration Suite. Before closing the event take the chance to meet the experts and to engage with your peers.

Check out the event page for further details and updates or simply register here (for SAP partners only).

Looking forward to seeing you all hopefully soon 😊

entry.title: Re: Change Record in S4HANA - A New Look to ECR/ECO in S4HANA
entry.content: Spectacular information, thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Configure Your Business Processes: An Introduction to SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Hi Fabian, I am not sure it is appropriate to ask here, but how can we access Central Business Configuration for our environment. We went productive with S/4 Cloud Public Edition many years ago already, but would like to add further business. I can not locate it in me.sap.com, and also not in Cloud ALM.

BR

Olof

entry.title: Extracting data from screenshots (with and without AI)
entry.content:
We've probably all been there dozens of times:
Having humbly requested information from a co-worker in the unshakable faith one possesses in their colleague's thoughtfulness, envisioning the forthcoming data neatly packaged in copy-paste-ready text. Only to see that faith crumble into a thousand despairing fragments upon the realization that they have gallantly sent... a screenshot?! 😕

While one surely could ask the aforementioned colleague to export the data properly on his end and resend it, there is no telling when he/she might come back. It might even be the next day. Trying to evangelize people into ideally not sending screenshots in such cases is only worth trying if you collaborate with them on a regular basis.

Transcribing the screenshot yourself is (assuming any reasonable amount of data - let's say 10+ material numbers) not an option as its dull & time consuming work and - let's be honest - feels like admitting defeat for any remotely tech-savvy human being.

So, what other options are there?
I tried the ones that seemed most obvious and accessible to me - so you don't have to.  

 

Screen grabber (TechSmith Snagit):

Allows you to extract text from a subselection or from whole screenshot (which even preserves table structures) or a sub-area.
While this method is rather quick, the accuracy is not convincing, as it has a tendency to confuse "O", "0" and  "Q".

 

MS OneNote: Copy Text from Picture 

Also OneNote offers a rudimentary extract text functionality. Here you can only extract all text from a whole image. OneNote's "Copy Text from Picture" displays the same tendency to confuse "O", "0" and  "Q". Table structures are not preserved. Instead it seems to interpret table borders crossing as the letter "L". It also occasionally omits portions of material numbers:

 

Mobile phone standard apps (Android Gallery) 

Turns out, even the standard "Gallery" app on Android 13+ is capable of extracting text. After taping the "Extract Text" button ("T" - bottom right), it will highlight all text it recognizes, whereafter you can select the text and copy it to the clipboard. Accurately selecting multiple bits of text is quite tedious though. Also it confused "Q" an "O" in one case.

Being an android person, I could not try this so far but the standard "Photos" app on iOS 15+ should have a similar capability called "Live Text" (has to be enabled in setting). 

 

 Large Language Models (LLMs)

Most of the younger large language models are also capable of processing images as input data - so why not give them a spin as well? At least with the examples I used, I found describing in natural language what to extract once and not having to select/copy/paste n times to be generally more convenient. While all LLMs I tried were able to "understand" the task and correctly identified the information to extract, there were some differences in accuracy. I used SAP AI Launchpad to access all of the LLMs except for CoPilot.

Anthropic Claude-3-Opus

...displayed a similar tendency to confuse "Q", "O" and "0" as observed with other methods:

 

Anthropic Claude-3-Haiku

While accurately recognizing "Q", "O" and "0", Haiku tends to (though rarely) sneak in additional digits:

 

 

Anthropic Claude-3.5-sonnet / OpenAI GPT 4o / Google Gemini-1.5-flash / MS Copilot

All these models accurately recognized & returned the text from my sample screenshots:

As this was my first time trying MS Copilot I was naively expecting it to be more deeply integrated in Windows and capable of accessing files and mails but you have to copy/paste the screenshot in the prompt (or upload it) just like with any other LLM: 

 

TL;DR

First of all, keep in mind the sample size of this little experiment is rather small (<5 screenshots total) so you likely will stumble on different issues.
Still - based on that small sample: Assuming all alternatives are accessible, the younger LLMs seem to offer the best mix of ease of use and accuracy. However, even those are not perfect: For example all LLMs missed suffixes in material numbers containing several spaces.
While their use definitely saves time, the results still should be double checked. 

PS: If you use other methods, feel free to mention them in a comment.

entry.title: Re: Migration - BP - Partner Fuctions - multiple contacts
entry.content:
Hi @Tiago_Pereira,

Contact persons are created as separate business partners using internal numbering. Since this new numbering is only created during the migration process, you can't use the contact person number as contact ID for partner functions in a single step.

To assign a contact person as partner, first create the supplier with the contact person but without the purchasing data. Then, use the Supplier - extend existing record by new org levels migration object as a second step to assign the purchasing data together with the contact person in partner capacity.

I would recommend reviewing the migration object documentation: Supplier

Hope this helps.

Arturo Montes de Oca

entry.title: Re: In asset master data quantity field need to be editab...
entry.content: You could change quantity with Quantity Adjustments within app 'Post Asset Acquisition - And Quantity Adjustment' F6488 (But AFAIK no check will be performed with this quantity)

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring metric threshold values setup and best practices
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We are configuring SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring. We are trying to decide the values for metric thresholds. What are the recommended best practice for the metric threshold limits for Alerting and notifications?

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring metric threshold valu...
entry.content:
This topic is addressed in detail in KBA 3553320 - How to decide metric threshold values for SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring? The KBA outlines some example scenarios using an imagined system landscape, and how their holistic information is used to make informed decisions when establishing metric threshold values.

In short, it is fine to use the pre-packaged "default" threshold values. But you will get maximum value out of Health Monitoring if you opt for custom values. To determine the Custom values, you must consider several factors in order to determine the importance of a metric, and thus what are appropriate threshold values. These factors can include:

  • The role the System+Client/Cloud Service plays in the landscape (Dev, Quality, etc.).
  • If the metric reaches "Critical", do you consider this a potential "business down" scenario? 
  • Do you need to be reactive at an earlier stage when a metric value starts to rise, or can you wait until much later for it to be addressed? 

Customers know their own landscape and business best, so they are equipped to outline these factors in great detail. Outlining these details for each System+Client/Cloud Service and metric allows you to holistically determine if a metric is important in the first place, and what values best represent the business needs.

SAP Support cannot provide bespoke detailed advice for Customers, as that is not a product issue/bug and instead a Consulting topic that must be addressed by an appropriate organization. If assistance is required, you will need to leverage a Consulting service to get further input.

entry.title: Compensation story report average
entry.content:
To replace dashboards that will be deprecated I'm just trying to do an average in a story report of Compa-ratio by Job Grade. 

The average doesn't calculate the same way as the average in a Tile I used before in my dashboard. The tile is correct and the story report shows the wrong average. There are no null or 0 values in my data.

See screenshot of attached configuration 

I'm using a Dimension to Measure Calculation for my Measure in this graph and the dimension is Current job grade.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Language of Folders
entry.content:
Hello @Stephen_Niemann,

I am Ascha and part of the virtual Customer Success Team at SAP Signavio.

After switching languages in SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub, it should also change across the sub solutions, so SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub and Process Manager. As it seems like this is not happening in your case, I can only re-call what @Shawn has already mentioned. Please open a case via SAP for Me to resolve this issue. Here you can find a step-by-step guideline on how to open a case.

For futher questions, feel free to comment here or to reach out to signavio_success@sap.com 

Thank you and kind regards

Ascha (as part of virtual Customer Success at SAP Signavio)

entry.title: Re: Replacement for CO06 in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello @t_jungesblut 

In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, backorder processing follows a different approach compared to the On-Premise system, where the CO06 transaction code is typically used to execute this process

Here are the steps for backorder processing

  1. Configure BOP Segment : Go to the Configure BOP Segment Fiori application and create two segments: one for "Win" and another for "Lose," applying the appropriate conditions. This setup allows you to reallocate the confirmed quantity from lower-priority customers sales orders to higher-priority customers sales orders.

     

     

  2. Configure BOP Variant : Go to the Configure BOP Variant Fiori application and create a variant as shown below. After creating the variant, click the Simulate button to validate it. If the simulation completes successfully, you can proceed to run backorder processing directly from the application. Otherwise, you can schedule a background job for this task.

  3. Schedule BOP Run : Go to the Schedule BOP Run Fiori Application and select ATP: Backorder Processing Default Template. Maintain the scheduling details based on your business requirements. In the subsequent step, choose the variant created in the previous step and execute the background job. Upon completion, the system reallocates the confirmed quantities from low-priority customer sales orders to high-priority customer sales orders.

Note : This is a sample approach for performing backorder processing. You can define your own conditions when configuring the BOP segment

Please Let me Know If you have any concern.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Datasphere Analytic Model oData: 404 Resource Not Found
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm trying to write the correct URL in order to retrieve data from an AM we have on our Datasphere tenant.

The AM has multiple filter variables, 2 of which are mandatory. The mandatory variables are START_DATE and END_DATE. I currently have my URL formed the following way:

https://<tenant>.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/<space>/<am_id>/<am_id>(START_DATE=2023-12-29,END_DATE=2023-12-29)/Set

This results in 

{"status":404,"message":"Resource not found"}

If I request the metadata for the AM, it works fine and returns the structure

https://<tenant>.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/<space>/<am_id>/$metadata

 What am I doing wrong to not get any data back? Are there any restrictions? How can I troubleshoot this?

Thanks

entry.title: link sales order reservation to refurbishment order
entry.content:
hello Gurus

I have the following scenario: we create a Sales order with a BOM, where the Header does not generate a requirement but the item does.

Our next step is to create a refurbishment order, where the material that generated the requirement is being issued, worked and returned in stock with a different number (the header material).

we link the refurbishment order to the sales order with the existing field in the refurbishment order.

I am trying to achieve the following: when I issue the material in the refurbishment order, I want the open requirement generated from the sales order to disappear from MD04.

is this possible?

Hi 

Once I had a similar problem. so I changed warehouse Process type setting and ticked select HU w/o storage process. Please you try for concerned WPT

 

It seems as per Jurgen reply it is standard behavior see below thread 

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/hu-and-product-task-for-outbound/qaq-p/13687440

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: Financial Accounting 4 Advanced Execution Service Scenario in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: Hi Stefan, unfortunately i was too quick saying all is clear. I set up a substitution and correctly derive a costing sheet which is event based. Then i settle my PM order to my service order item by cost element (kostenartengerecht). Yet, no surcharge through the costing sheet is applied. I hoped this would happen event based but it didnt and i dont find an app to execute surcharge calculation on the service order item. Are settled cost not recognized on the service order item (note: for manually posted costs through FB01 the costing sheet pulls the surcharge). Thanks again

entry.title: Anticipo Proveedor con OC de Activo Fijo
entry.content:
Hola,

Estoy tratando de crear un anticipo a un proveedor que tiene una orden de compra con imputacion de anticipo. el activo se ha creado como maquinaria en curso y la orden de compra se asocio este activo fijo creado, hasta ahi no problema, pero cuando quiero crear el anticipo me salta el siguiente error y no puedo continuar:

Diagnóstico

El número del activo fijo imputado   en el documento de compras

  • difiere del número de activo fijo introducido manualmente o
  • no puede copiarse, ya que el campo "Número de activo fijo" se ha suprimido para la cuenta en cuestión.

Actividades en el sistema

En el primer caso aparece una advertencia indicando diferentes imputaciones, en el segundo caso aparece un mensaje de error, ya que el número de activo fijo se debe tomar del pedido, pero está suprimido el campo correspondiente.

Procedimiento

En el caso de una advertencia se debe verificar cuál es el número del activo fijo para este anticipo.

En el caso de una advertencia se debe seleccionar otro indicador de operación en cuenta de mayor especial para anticipos, para que esté permitido la imputación de un número de activo fijo en la cuenta de mayor o se debe modificar o borrar el número del documento de compras indicado. Sólo están permitidos los documentos de compras que no se han imputado en activo fijo.

Nº mensaje: F5378

Si reviso la determinacion de cuenta y la GL asociada para el movimiento de anticipo tengo la cuenta:

 

Como puedo solucionar el error?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management Sourcing and Procurement - Purchase Contract Management #downpayment

entry.title: Re: I How to extract the BP address information?
entry.content:
Hi colleague,

Hope you're fine.

I can't suggest which is CDS view is most suitable for your requirement, although we have a lot of CDS view implemented for this specific query.

Can you go through Business Accelerator and find them:

CDS Views | CDS Views | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

These CDS View have all the technical details for being used.

Best Regards,
Erick Goulardt
SAP Support

entry.title: Standard class has deleted CL_FS4BPU_EMPL_MINIMASTER_API
entry.content:
Class "CL_FS4BPU_EMPL_MINIMASTER_API" seems not to exist anymore on SAP S/4HANA 2023 (it previously existed in 2021 release).

  • Is there a direct replacement for this removed object?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Standard class has deleted CL_FS4BPU_EMPL_MINIMASTER_API
entry.content: Indeed, class "CL_FS4BPU_EMPL_MINIMASTER_API" was replaced by the class "CL_FS4BPU_MAINTAIN_API"

entry.title: Re: SAC: date type
entry.content: Please check the unit formatting in both the story and the model. If the value is in billions or millions, it may round up, which is why your value is showing as zero.

entry.title: A Centralized Job Monitor in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
I am excited to introduce the upcoming release of the Job Monitor, which will be available with our 2025 QRC1 release!

With the Job Monitor, it will be much easier for business users and admin users to gain insights about the status of background jobs and overall processes.

 

The Challenges Today

Currently, the lack of a monitor for multi actions results in a lack of transparency in multi actions execution.  

Fragmented monitors for data actions, data import / export, and smart predictive make it challenging to monitor jobs across the system. 

When a user triggers a multi action, they must navigate between different monitors to check the status, making the process cumbersome. Job status, duration, and error analysis are scattered across various monitors, making it hard to interpret the end-to-end status.

Additionally, when users have questions about some long-running jobs and want to determine the root cause of the extended duration (e.g. system busyness), it’s hard to get a holistic overview of job execution at any specific time.

 

Job Monitor

The Job Monitor provides a centralized location for overseeing SAC background jobs. The QRC1 2025 release will include monitoring capabilities for data action jobs and multi action jobs. Further jobs will be integrated into the Job Monitor in future releases.

With the Job Monitor, users can easily monitor, troubleshoot, and analyze the performance of SAC jobs, helping them to streamline their workflow and optimize the configuration of data actions and multi actions.

A wide variety of filter functions allow users to flexibly search for jobs from various perspectives. For example:

  • Use the “Started on” filter to focus on jobs within a specific period
  • Apply the “Total Duration” filter to review long-running jobs
  • Utilize the “Status” filter to analyze failed jobs
  • Check scheduled jobs by Calendar using the “Started From” filter

The Job Monitor provides a comprehensive 360° view for jobs, including details such as where the job was triggered from, the total execution time, who triggered the job and when it was triggered. On the detailed page, you can dive deeper into the job to see a breakdown of the duration, records changed, parameter values, and more.

Various monitoring pages provide insights tailored to different job types and varying levels.

  • Dedicated monitor pages for data actions and multi actions provide focused insights for each job type.
  • The “All jobs” page provides a thorough overview for all data actions and multi actions jobs.
  • The “Main jobs” page offers a clear list of first-level jobs, including the first-level data actions and multi actions. The data actions embedded within multi actions or first-level data actions are not displayed on this page.

Advanced text search enables a global search within the Job Monitor, including both the job list and the detailed information pages. This functionality allows you to effortlessly find any job-related information, such as jobs that use a specific dimension member in their parameters.

Different user roles are supported.

  • Business users can view all jobs that they started, jobs started from tasks shared with them in the Calendar, and data actions jobs whose target versions they own.
  • Admins or Process Owners have full access to monitor all jobs, allowing them to oversee the job status of the entire tenant.

The performance of job list display has been enhanced largely for both initial loading and subsequent paging compared with previous Data Action Monitor.

 

Note: The Job Monitor is the successor of the Data Action Monitor.

 

Multi Actions Monitor within Job Monitor

The Multi Actions Monitor within the Job Monitor offers full transparency and traceability for multi actions jobs.

On the dedicated Multi Actions page, you can get an overview of all multi actions jobs. Business users can view all multi actions jobs they own. Admin users can see all multi actions jobs across the tenant.

By selecting a specific job, you can have a deeper look at its detailed status. This includes execution progress, step-by-step job details, records imported, records changed, parameter values, and more in one place.   

The detailed status of the data action step is integrated into the Multi Actions Monitor, reducing the need to navigate between different monitors.

For the data import step within multi actions, that have rejected data, you can also easily download the rejected data from the Job Monitor without navigating to Data Management page. However, if you need more details about import job settings, you can still navigate to the Data Management page.

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, the QRC1 2025 release of SAP Analytics Cloud introduces the Job Monitor, a centralized tool that simplifies job monitoring for business users and admin users. It consolidates fragmented monitors, enhancing transparency and efficiency.

entry.title: Alternatative solution to run or execute old report painter GR55 on SAP FIORI Launchpad?
entry.content:
GR55 Report Painter Execution is a GUI classic transacction that includes several reports from legacy, some of them were created ad hoc and I do not want it to created from the scratch using new technologies as CDS view at the moment. 

Is there a option to call this reports from FIORI?

For example: To run old profitability analysis report (KE30) SAP FIORY LIBRARY it has the catalogue  SAP_TC_FIN_CO_BE_APPS

entry.title: Re: HU warehouse task for outbound scenario
entry.content: Hi Rashid, No we dont have any such settings in Storage type.

entry.title: Re: View the execution plan of SAC widgets/ charts
entry.content:
Hi check this:,

1.Filters or dimensions in the query might not align with the available data.
Dynamic input variables may not have valid values.

2.Ensure the query is valid and aligns with the model's data structure.

Remove filters temporarily to test data retrieval.

3.Try to comment out unnecessary scripting part.

entry.title: Build exception aggregation property in SAC using Flat file
entry.content: Build exception aggregation property in SAC using Flat file

entry.title: Re: Kernel Patch to support TLSv1.3 - SAP Note 3318423
entry.content: I would say the fact that TLS 1.3 handshakes can usually complete the handshake with 1 fewer round trip than in TLS 1.2 is a material benefit.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Analytic Model oData: 404 Resource Not Found
entry.content:
Hi @mitko1994,

If your parameters START_DATE and ENDDATE supporting multiple values then your API call would be like 

https://<tenant>.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/<space>/<am_id>/<am_id>(START_DATE='eq 2023-12-29',END_DATE='eq 2023-12-29')/Set

Please check out Set Values for Input Parameters / Variables 

Thanks,
Tuncay

entry.title: Full Tax Amount (100%) Transfer To Tax Account
entry.content:
Hi All 

Is there a way to transfer full tax amount (100%) tax account? For Example-My invoice is of 100 USD.

Vendor-100 USD

Acc ABC-75 USD (Tax 100%)

Acc CDE-25 USD (Tax 20%)

After posting we want to see it like below-

Vendor-100 USD

Tax Acc ABC-75 USD

Acc CDE-21 USD

Tax Acc-4 USD

At present 20% tax code is working fine however 100% tax code is not transferring full amount to tax acc rather than it is only doing 50%. This is input tax & I tried playing with NVV too along with input tax but still no success.

Regards

Pankaj

entry.title: Re: New Learning Experience Library item visibility delay...

KBA 3389475 was broken down into 3 separate ones, see below:

3511497 - New Learning Experience: New Home Page

3511612 - New Learning Experience: Enhanced Search

3511498 - New Learning Experience: Recommendations

entry.title: LO_BCS Creates folder in client's outlook for PDF print.
entry.content:
I am using LO_BCS to send an attachment of Smartform output print which is nothing but a PDF file, however this attachment creates extra folder in Outlook, which requires one extra click to open PDF file.

Is there any solution to send an attachment without this extra folder?       

 

 

Actual PDF after clicking the folder.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network For Logistics - Carrier Side ANSI-X12 EDI Setup and Testing for Road
entry.content:
Hi Jeff!

On the L11, you are showing in the example(GEBO 214 Demo.txt) the carrier should be sending back the reference number with the 06 qualifier. One of our carriers had to send back that L11 with a T7 Qualifier for other customers. Can they can do either?

L11*QW9_172*06~OrL11*GTT*T7~

Thanks

Ana

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Transformation Center - Digital Blueprint

entry.title: restriccion en Oferta de Ventas
entry.content:
Estimados (a):

Quisiera ver si me pueden ayudar, necesito validar en la oferta de venta en Sap Business One que el usuario al momento de ingresar una ofera de venta y no grabarla en el sistema este no tenga la opcion de imprimirla oferta ni guardarla como pdf. Tengo un campo adicional llamdo kilometraje (llamado en tabla U_MMM_Kms)  que este campo al no tener datos bloquea lo que necesito pero al ingresar un dato destraba todo y puden generar las ofertas sin grabarlas en el sistema. Estoy tratando de hacerlo con un Store Procedure pero no me funciona esto es lo que eh hecho.

/*Valida Impresion OV*/

IF :object_type = '23' AND (:transaction_type = 'A' OR :transaction_type = 'U') THEN
-- Verificar si la oferta de ventas está grabada en el sistema
SELECT COUNT(*) INTO temp_var_2_jls
FROM OQUT T0
WHERE T0."DocEntry" = :list_of_cols_val_tab_del;

-- Si no se encuentra la oferta grabada (COUNT(*) = 0), bloquear la impresión
IF temp_var_2_jls = 0 THEN
-- Error personalizado para bloquear la impresión
error := -50002; -- Código de error personalizado
error_message := N'La oferta de ventas no está grabada. No se puede imprimir.';
END IF;
END IF;

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network For Logistics - Carrier Side ANSI-X12 EDI Setup and Testing for Road
entry.content:
Hi Ana,

Segment L11*GTT*T7~ is used for carriers that are integrating with shipper's running Global Track & Trace (GTT). I should have mentioned in the above that the 214 mentioned was for FC Tracking an NOT GTT. For carriers creating 214s for GTT they would need to send the following segments:

L11*<Sender System ID>*06~
L11*FT1_SHIPMENT*8X~
L11*GTT*T7~

This is assuming you are tracking by FO using the standard GTT model ( where FT1_SHIPMENT comes from ). All these segments are required to be sent if the shipper is using GTT.

GTT also uses different stop Ids where are formatted like so: <Fright Order #><0001> for stop Id 1. So if your FO# was 63000001234 the stop Id for stop #1 would be 630000012340001.

Now, on top of this GTT supports the 213 - which can be sent from the shipper's GTT to the carrier - it's basically an initiate tracking request to the carrier and has all the info needed in it about location Ids, etc. That said, most carriers don't support the 213 - I've only run into one that does.

I'll try to update this blog or create a new blog to cover GTT EDI integration. You can find all the specs for GTT EDI integration here: https://help.sap.com/docs/business-network-global-track-and-trace?task=integrate_task

entry.title: Re: How to Post Cost to a WBS Element for a Balance Sheet...
entry.content: Thank you, how do I go about updating these values?

entry.title: Long time to run a report in VS2022 the first time

It now takes 2 minutes to a report the first time.  Whichever we select to report run takes 2 minutes and then any subsequent reports run as normal.  This started about 3 weeks ago.

Any thoughts?

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:

Looks like a recurrence of the issue fixed in note 2876817 (V_BTXRATE & V_BTXOVFR: not saving Experience Rate and incorrect number of digits for Tax Type). We are on SP level 608 D7 and this note is already in the system. 

Anyone is noticing this?

Thanks
Chandra

entry.title: Re: How to redeem a campaign code to get a free SAP Certification exam attempt and schedule your exa
entry.content:

I have already registered and confirmed for digital skills initiative and shared my learning journey completion in November. But I haven’t received the free certificate redemption code yet. Could you please advise. I have also mailed to digitalskills@sap.com several times. Please your response will be of much help. 

Thanks and regards,

Jenifer

entry.title: Re: Compensation Route Map
entry.content:
Hi @vinolithoppil 

Compensation route maps can have an unlimited number of steps that can reference any hierarchy (even a mix of line manager hierarchy with 2nd manager hierarchy).

However I would advise against configuring more than 2-3 actual steps and use Executive Review to valide large scope of employees towards the end of the process. You can leverage the 2H 2024 enhancement to trigger executive review scopes automatically based on route map step (see first paragraph in this blog with demo recording).

Also one important clarification is that the compensation module is a manager centric module which means that when defining actual approval step the worksheets with employees in them are sent to approvers tied to the worksheet planner as defined through method of planning. Which means that we cannot build an approval step 2 where employee A goes to HR 1 and employee B goes to HR 2. They will all go to the same HR that must be tied to the worksheet planner. Which is why we usually use Executive Review to build this as it provides more flexibility in defining a scope that is not based on the planner.

 

There is a section about route map leading practice at the bottom of this blog: https://blogs.sap.com/2020/09/12/sap-model-company-for-compensation/

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements app shows wrong decimal separators
entry.content: Hi, I can only confirm with the newer SAPUI version (it was 1.108.28 if I'm not wrong), my problem described above is solved - the amounts are displayed correctly. I can change the decimal characters in my Fiori settings and it's displayed correctly in any case.

I am using SmartFilterBar & smartTable and created custom app , All the filters are coming using CDS annotation .

Now in controller when I get the object of SmartFilterBar and use like below
oSmartFilterBar.getControlByKey("plant").getValue();

I am able to get the value for normal filters using the key but if I try to get value or object of AnalyticalParameters it does not work .
If I try to get object AnalyticalParameters using key it returns null.

Please guide me how I can get the object and value of AnalyticalParameters


Regards,
Piyush

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone: Integrate SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud content to SAP Mobile Start
entry.content:
Hi @Amith_Nair ,

I followed the steps from the blog on integrating SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud content into SAP Build Work Zone, and we are successfully able to retrieve roles and role-specific tiles. Users are authenticated through our identity provider without any issues. However, when users click on the tiles, they are prompted again for credentials, and none of the credentials are working.

It seems like the issue could be related to the Single Sign-On (SSO) or trust configuration between the identity provider and SAP Build Work Zone. Has anyone encountered this issue or have any suggestions on how to resolve it? Any help would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!"

Regards,
Nithesh

entry.title: Call SAP Digital Manufacturing Production Processes via REST API
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Digital Manufacturing Production Processes can be initiated out of the box in many different ways, including automatic triggers and POD action buttons for example. But calling production processes directly from the external world, including from 3rd party solutions, is also a common implementation requirement.

Although there is currently no public API available to call production processes from the external world, there is an internal API available that can do that. In the blog post, I will show you how to do it, but keep in mind that, as this is an internal API, availability, support, stability and compatibility can change anytime without further notice.

Implementation Steps

First, you need to Create a Service Instance and Service Key in SAP Digital Manufacturing Subaccount and get public-api-endpoint, clientid, clientsecret and url values.

Then, create, save and deploy your production process, but make sure you click "..." > 'Edit Header' in the top right corner of the PPD and switch ON 'Publish to Service Registry' option.

Then, go to Manage Service Registry app, filter, find, select your production process and get 'URL / Path' value.

Now, you can call your production processes from the external world via REST, using <url>+'/oauth/token', 'clientid' and 'clientsecret' values from the Service Key for OAUTH 2.0 client credential authentication, and 'public-api-endpoint' from the Service Key + 'URL / Path' from Manage Service Registry app as URL. See a Postman example below:

* For 'Access Token URL', use the 'url' field from service key and add '/oauth/token' to the end. A typical access token service URL, for example, should look like: https://{$Subdomain}.authentication.xxxx.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token

Which runs successfully and returns  "processInstanceName": "TriggerAlert#2537", which can be also seen in SAP Digital Manufacturing at Monitor Production Processes app.

You can map production process input parameters using JSON, example:

Which can be seen in Monitor Production Processes as well:

Conclusion

Using an internal API, you can trigger SAP Digital Manufacturing Production Processes from outside, but keep in mind that, as this is an internal API, availability, support, stability and compatibility can change anytime without further notice.

The scenario described in this blog post is as simple as possible for educational purposes, but can be further extended and leveraged in more complex scenarios easily.

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: How to create inbound api endpoint for SAP DMC
entry.content:
Hi, see: Call SAP Digital Manufacturing Production Processes via REST API.
Hope it helps,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Activation of SAP Mobile Start Application on SAP BTP Neo
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

Is there anyway to activate Mobile application 'SAP Mobile Start' using SAP BTP Neo Cockpit?
How to get SAP BTP Cloud Foundry access ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Activation of SAP Mobile Start Application on SAP BTP...
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP Mobile Start is not available on Neo.
To get access to Cloud Foundry, you can just create a CF subaccount in your BTP Global Account. Please see:

2786277 - How to create a subaccount within a Global Account in SAP BTP

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Streamlining Time Sheet Approvals in SuccessFactors: Time Sheet Approval Center
entry.content:
@STtiltsch I was struggling with the same issue for a moment. Not able to see the direct link on the home page, until I realized there is a limit of 16 direct links on the Quick Actions.

So what I have done is just moving the Time Sheet Approval Center direct link between the 16 first direct links on the Manage Home Page tool to make sure it is displayed.

entry.title: Re: "TYPE zcl_epm_products_mpc_base" is unknown
entry.content:
This is a very old question but it's the only one that currently shows up in Google when searching for the mystery "TYPE ZCL... is unknown" error message.

I found that this message can pop up when ZCL... itself has just been updated (even if it's active) and you are also updating another object that uses ZCL... . If you're banging your head on the desk / wall / other hard object trying to figure out what is wrong with your syntax (class is active, method name is correct, etc.), there is likely nothing wrong with your code and it's just ABAP / IDE being weird. If this happens, try closing the object you're editing and the class ZCL... in question. (You could activate the class again for good measure.)
Then re-open the object where you previously got this error and run syntax check. The error should no longer appear.

If "have you tried turning it on and off again" doesn't help, then check if there is indeed something wrong with the code or ZCL in question. But I found that this message can also be a false alarm and is incredibly confusing.

entry.title: Re: Inventory Accounting with Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content: @WeekendVampire You won't be able to pass "E" stock or "Q" stock via the intercompany sales/stock transfer processes at the moment. These processes only work in a make-to-stock scenario.

entry.title: Leap Year
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

As you know, the current year (2024) was a leap year. However, SAP has forgotten to adapt the corresponding calendars for some Calendars other than the Gregorian calendar, such as the Japanese or Solar calendars. 

As seen in the attached screenshot, the last day of the current Solar Year (30.12.1403) does not exist in the Calendar, which should be the same day as 20.03.2025, whereas the next year (1404) is falsely considered as a leap year!

 

Does anyone have an idea how to solve this problem?

Thanks and best Regards
Vahmani

entry.title: Re: Memory issue in SAP application server
entry.content:
Hello,

These 30GB-40GB that you are seeing at OS level are likely the SAP shared memory areas, which are used by all work processes in parallel. So, it is not that each work process is using 30GB, they are all using the same 30GB block.

This SAP Support Content page can help to clarify this.

With the above in mind, please share details about the actual issue being observed, so we can try to assist you :-).

@rosette_vaughn2 not sure what you mean by "not allowing new entry".  Post a screenshot of the error you are seeing. You need to use T5UTT for maintenance, not the view table (V_T5UTT).

Thanks

Chandra

entry.title: Re: Single Sign Error- Failed to connect to a local Secure Login Client Web Adapter.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Transformation Center - Digital Blueprint
entry.content:
Hi @johnny999 , everything described here in this Blog is based on Data Collection that is done using the SAP Readiness Check for Usage & Data Profiling. The RC UDP needs to be executed in ECC system. After the data collection, you can download the results as a zip file and upload them to SAP BTC. On this basis, you can then create the digital blueprint and carry out all the scoping activities mentioned above. No additional system scan needed.

But there is indeed a system scan in SAP BTC which is used to determine all tables that are not covered in the SAP delivered Transformation Object content and the Readiness Check. You can find all details on that in this Blog from @ChristianeStrasserGößler : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/completing-the-digital-blueprint-with-the-system-scan/ba-p/13805578

entry.title: Re: Activity Area in Extended Warehouse Management
entry.content: Its very usuful information.

entry.title: Re: LSMW issue for transaction CG02BD

entry.title: SmartFilterBar Date field default value with sign greater than or equal to
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working on a SmartFilterBar with Date field and trying to populate default value with operation / option like shown in image (Greater than or equal to today's date). I tried few ways but didn't work. Can someone please help by pointing detailed way to achieve this?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Reprinting a Packing List with Alternative Printers and Managing PPF Condition Records
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In our SAP EWM system, we trigger the printing of the packing list via a PPF action. The PPF is executed automatically once the staging activity for an Outbound Delivery Order (ODO) is completed.

Background:

Sometimes, the printer maintained in the condition records for the PPF action is broken or unavailable. To address this, we currently use the monitor (e.g., transaction /SCWM/MON) to reprint the ODO, which allows users to preview the PDF and select a different printer manually.

Questions:

  1. Is there another way for users to manage output devices when a reprint is needed, other than through the monitor?
  2. If I configure multiple condition records for the same PPF action and assign different printers to each condition record, is there a way for the system to:
    • Follow an access sequence or logic?
    • Attempt to use the next condition record (printer) automatically if the initial one fails?

I want to ensure that the reprint process can handle printer issues more dynamically, rather than relying solely on manual intervention.

Any insights, best practices, or alternative solutions would be highly appreciated!

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: Product master update EWM error /SCMB/MD_LOCK101
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

 

I try to update a material in embeded EWM for the first time but i'm getting the next error

I found the next SAP note "2493879 - Master data lock errors during product master update". That says is possible that i miss locking parameters config

 

Th problem is that i cannot find it in my system. In the SAP note says that is "SPRO -> SCM Basis -> Master Data -> Define Parameters for Master Data Locking Concept" but in my system on that path i don't see it

Why that SPRO path is not there? it was changed?

entry.title: How to become a functional consultant? Student trying to become a consultant!

But I do not have coding experience or either any certification in SAP but I do have learning experience in some of the modules like SD. WM, procurement and FI little bit. I am completely non technical background that's why I seeking for help to enhance my knowledge! 

In terms of entry level, where should I look for opportunity like any specific companies or industry? 

Best Regards,

Gokul

We have maintained number range object and number range number in serial number profile as we want to generate serial number and equipment master as per number range provided by client.

Issue: During goods receipt in EWM, we are able to create serial numbers automatically in inbound delivery, however goods receipt fails on ERP side with message that "Equipment Number not in external number range".

Appreciate your help on this issue.

Thanks

Amit

entry.title: Study Guide for SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411
entry.content:
Abstract

This study guide is designed to aid in your preparation for the SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud exam (C_C4H32_2411). This certification is intended for professionals who possess fundamental and core knowledge in SAP Commerce Cloud and can function effectively as part of a project team under guidance. It is ideal for business users seeking an entry-level qualification to work with SAP Commerce Cloud.

Overview

Before we begin, it's important to note that proper preparation for the exam is highly recommended. To ensure success, consider attending the following:

  • C4H320 - SAP Commerce Cloud Business User Training: This course contains ALL the essential content required for passing the exam. Enrolling in this course is highly beneficial.
  • Hands-on Practice System for C4H320: If attending the C4H320 training is not feasible, you can independently book this system. It offers practice opportunities with all the hands-on exercises from the training and provides access to all the training material PDFs.
  • Business User Certification Preparation Live Session: This live session features an instructor who reviews key exam topics and can directly address many of your related questions.

This exam covers the following 6 topic areas:

  • Product Content Management
  • Web Content Management
  • Commerce Management
  • Order Management & Customer Support
  • Integrations
  • Essential Foundation

Let's explore each exam topic area and its essential checkpoints in detail.

All Topics Walk-Through

1. Product Content Management (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should aim to understand the following:

  • Backoffice Product Cockpit Features: Gain a comprehensive understanding of all views (list/grid), functionalities, and toolbar actions, with particular emphasis on synchronization, Excel import/export, and cloning.
  • Product Concepts: Familiarize yourself with products, categories, catalogs, and catalog versions. Assess catalog management involving single or multiple catalog versions and understand performance characteristics.
  • Product Modeling Approaches: Explore type systems, basic and multi-dimensional variants, and classification systems.
  • Media Management: Focus on media containers and media formats, as well as conversion and storage processes.

The essentials links includes:

  • Backoffice Product Content Management - User Guide
  • Working with Catalogs
  • Product Modelling
  • Synchronizing Catalogs
  • Classification
  • Modeling Product Variants
  • B2B Multi-Dimensional Products
  • Digital Asset Management
  • Media Conversion

2. Web Content Management (11-20%)

In this topic area, focus on understanding the following:

  • Concepts of Base Stores and Related Items: Grasp the relationships among base stores, sites, product catalogs, and content catalogs.
  • CMS Components: Explore page templates, content slots, and CMS restrictions.
  • SmartEdit Features: Become familiar with various views such as pages, navigation, and homepage, along with the modes (preview | basic edit | advanced edit | versioning) for editing and versioning features.
  • Personalization Concepts: Understand segments, triggers, actions, and the configuration of personalization modules via SmartEdit and Backoffice.
  • Commerce Customization: Learn principles and applications of personalized search and promotions.
  • Composable Storefront: Understand the working principles and the communication process between composable storefront and SAP Commerce Cloud.

The essentials links are:

  • Overview of the CMS2 Data Model
  • Setting Up a Basic Web Site in the Backoffice
  • SmartEdit Features
  • Personalization Module Features
  • SAP Commerce Cloud, Composable Storefront Documentation

3. Commerce Management (11-20%)

In this topic area, your should develop a thorough understanding of the following:

  • Cart and Checkout Management: Familiarize yourself with the cart handling process and the various types of checkout options available.
  • Pricing Mechanism and Functionalities: Delve into the aspects of a price factory, price rows, and the evaluation priorities associated with multiple price rows linked to the same product.
  • Search Management: Understand the integration with the default search engine, focusing on index management, search box configuration, facets configuration, and the Adaptive Search perspective in Backoffice (including search profiles, activation sets, promoting items and boost rules, etc).
  • Promotion and Coupon Management: Learn about the setup and configuration of promotions, including conditions and actions, the evaluation process, the distinctions between single-code and multi-code coupons, and the role of coupons in promotions.
  • B2B Commerce Models: Gain insight into typical B2B data models, such as B2B users, units, cost centers, budgets, and credit limits. Also, understand B2B process support, including order approval with B2B permissions, quote negotiations, and punchout integration processes.

Here are the important links:

  • Search and Navigation Module Features
  • Adaptive Search Module Features
  • Promotion Engine Features
  • Coupon Features
  • Price Calculation
  • B2B Commerce Features
  • B2B PunchOut Module

4. Order Management and Customer Support (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should focus on:

  • Payment Integration: Understand SOP (Silent Order Post) and HOP (Hosted Order Page)
  • Basic Order Management Extensions: Explore functions such as standard order fulfillment processes, order replenishment, order splitting, and stock information management.
  • Order Management Services Module: Understand the configuration of various aspects, including Available to Promise (ATP), sourcing, and the default fulfillment workflow involving picking, packing, labeling, and shipment.
  • Assisted Service and Customer Service Modules: Learn about the features of the Assisted Service Module (such as creating customer profiles, managing shopping carts, and placing/canceling/returning orders) for storefronts, and the Customer Service Module (including managing tickets, approving reviews, and generating GDPR reports) in Backoffice.
  • Fraud Detection: Grasp the principles of the "game of points" system and the default symptom types used in fraud detection.

The important links are:

  • Base Commerce Module Features (Order Related Ones)
  • Order Management Features
  • Assisted Service Module Features
  • Customer Service Module Features

5. Integrations (<10%)

In this topic area, you should understand the following:

  • SAP Integration Pillars: Focus on the integration of data, process, and feature aspects.
  • Integration Scenarios: Examine different setups such as loosely coupled, pick-and-choose, and tightly coupled configurations.
  • General Integration Tools: Learn about various tools and services, including Impex, Cloud Hotfolder, OCC web services, integration API module, and Webhooks.
  • Integration Extension Pack: Identify the default support available for SAP solutions and systems.
  • SAP BTP Integration Suite and Kyma Runtime: Explore their features and underlying concepts.

More information in:

  • Introduction to SAP Commerce Integrations
  • Integration API Features
  • Cloud Hot Folders
  • SAP Commerce Cloud, Integration Extension Pack
  • SAP Back-End Integration Overview

6. Essential Foundations (21-30%)

In this topic area, you should understand the following:

  • Concepts of Extensions and Modules: Familiarize yourself with how extensions and modules function within the system.
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Architecture and Components: Gain insight into the structure and individual components of SAP Commerce Cloud.
  • License Management: Understand the nuances of using/managing temporary and production licenses.
  • Monthly Update Scheme**: Learn about the versioning system and the purpose, release process, and contents of each update, and how to stay current and supported.
  • SAP Commerce Cloud in the Public Cloud: Explore the differences and benefits compared to on-premises solutions, including environment setup and scaling support.
  • Backoffice General Functionalities: Get to know the general functions and GUI components, such as the top menu bar.
  • Management of the Validation Framework via Backoffice: Understand how to manage validation rules and processes.
  • Cronjobs: Learn about the definition, application, and execution of cronjobs.
  • Impex and Distributed ImpEx: Understand the definition, application, and execution of Impex, along with the concept and advantages of distributed ImpEx.
  • Workflows and Collaboration Center: Learn how to define, manage, and monitor workflows within Backoffice.
  • Security Management:
    • User Management (principals, user groups)
    • Type-based Access Rights
    • Restrictions and Personalization Rules
    • HAC Access Customization
    • Password Policies and Change Audits
  • GDPR Support and Components: Understand the features and components that ensure GDPR compliance.
  • Intelligent Selling Services: identify the basic features and integration with SAP Commerce Cloud, especially the types of product recommendations provided.
  • Out-of-the-Box Features of B2C and B2B in Composable Storefront**: Familiarize yourself with the ready-to-use features available for B2C/B2B scenarios.

Here are the essential links:

  • Data Management with ImpEx
  • The Cronjob Service
  • Data Validation Framework
  • Workflow and Collaboration
  • Access Rights
  • Restrictions
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Architecture
  • Environment Components
  • License
  • Supported Releases
  • Update Releases
  • Intelligent Selling Services
     

Summary

By thoroughly exploring the above topic areas and the included links, you'll be well-prepared for the SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud exam.

Good luck on your certification journey, and may your hard work pay off!

entry.title: Re: Issue with Creating an Situation Type for Outbound Delivery Notifications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Hi @kk_19, please explain the business process you'd like to get accomplish, not just technical implementation details. For outbound delivery, we do have APIs available (at api.sap.com) that appear to work well for most customers. "Updates" of outbound deliveries, what exactly do you mean by those? Lost shipments? Status changes during shipping? ASN might be covering situations like those if appropriate for the contractual obligation

entry.title: Error when modifying SY-SUBRC in S/4HANA Cloud Public systems using Eclipse debugger
entry.content:
Hi guys,

When I try to modify system variable SY-SUBRC in S/4HANA Cloud Public systems using Eclipse debugger, I get the following error message:

Important points to mention:

  • I did a test in 3 different Cloud Public systems, all of them showed the same error.
  • I tried on some On-Premise systems and was allowed to modify the variable without any issues.
  • I searched for posts or SAP Notes talking about any restriction, but didn't find anything.
  • I'm allowed to modify any other variable

Does anyone know anything about this issue?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Bug with Build Apps Community Edition Page disappearing
entry.content:
Every time I try to edit my page variable for my "recipeEdit" page, the page disappears. The page is still there, and I can make changes to it, but the UI is gone. I can still see the page in preview with new changes. When I restore, the page comes back. But as soon as I try to change a page variable, it goes away again. Any Ideas?

 

Ever

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration Cloud Foundry: Integration Content API not available
entry.content:
Hi Experts

I hope all of you are doing well.

I have stumbled across the Integration Content API for SAP Cloud Integration, and it looks really promising:

https://api.sap.com/api/IntegrationContent/overview, 

I have tried to access this API from my local SAP Cloud Integration tenant, which is running as part of Integration Suite on Cloud Foundry. For the record I have not enabled SAP API Management, but according to most of the blogs I have read it does not seem to me that it is required. 

When I try to call the API endpoint just to see if I can reach it, I receive an HTTP 404 - Not Found. The URL I tried is:
https://<sub account name>.integrationsuite.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/IntegrationPackages

I have also used the following URL, and this gives me an Odata response with a bunch of Entity Sets, but the entity sets related to SAP Cloud Integration are not listed (for example the IntegrationPackages entity set is not there).

https://<sub account name>.integrationsuite.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1

I have gone through countless amounts of SAP blogs and SAP Help documents, and they all highlight that the API should just be available for SAP Cloud Integration with no instructions to enable the integration content to be made available. This makes me think that I have the incorrect DNS\host name in the API URL I am trying to call.

So, to my question: Do I need to do anything specific to enable this Integration Content API for my local SAP Cloud Integration tenant?

If yes, will someone please be so kind to provide me with instructions on what is required?

If no, will someone please explain to me what is the correct URL I should use to access this API for my local SAP Cloud Integration tenant?

SAP Integration Suite
